name: Conor McGregor

qualityWinLedger.context: Prime featherweight champion and all-time great.
Elite featherweight contender on short notice.
Early win over future all-time featherweight great.
Strong featherweight contender before later lightweight peak.
High-level rivalry win at welterweight; not title-contender context.
Early UFC win with limited quality value.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Nate I did not end it because Conor recovered with Nate II and Eddie. Khabib is endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: McGregor won the rematch and then captured the lightweight title.

shortCase: Conor is the star-power and iconic-moment case: the Aldo knockout, the Alvarez masterclass, the double-champ moment, and the biggest cultural impact in UFC history.
bestArgument: Conor wins comparisons when peak fame, historic moments, and two-division star power outweigh deeper but less spectacular resumes.
counterArgument: Conor’s argument is impact. If the debate is iconic moments, star power, and changing the business, he is in a class almost by himself.
whyRankedHere: McGregor ranks here because his best UFC moments are enormous. The Aldo knockout, Alvarez masterclass, Mendes win, Holloway win, and Poirier early win make his peak impact impossible to ignore.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because the long-term UFC body of work is short. No title defenses, inactivity, and later losses keep the scoring honest even though the cultural impact is unmatched.
finalTakeaway: Conor is the UFC iconic-moment king: massive peak impact and history-changing star power, but a shorter and less defended resume than the deeper GOAT cases.
